methods.description: Collection of core GEOFAR KF16 from the Mid Atlantic Ridge and comparison with published data to trace the varying position of the STG and influence of subtropical and temperate waters.

steps.description: For our study, we used core GEOFAR KF16 taken at 38°N, south of the Azores Islands at the Mid Atlantic Ridge, from 3060 m water depth.
steps.searchable_summary: collect core sample GEOFAR KF16 from specified location

steps.description: Results are compared to published data from the tropical and subpolar North Atlantic along the warm water route.
steps.searchable_summary: compare core sample data with published datasets

methods.description: Analysis of Mg/Ca ratios in foraminifera to estimate water temperatures using species-specific calibrations.

steps.description: Mg/Ca analyses followed the same procedure as in Repschläger et al., 2015, with a 2 σ STD of +/-0.20 mmol/mol Mg/Ca for G. ruber w. and +/-0.14 mmol/mol Mg/Ca for G. truncatulinoides.
steps.searchable_summary: perform Mg/Ca analysis on foraminifera samples

steps.description: To convert Mg/Ca values into water temperature estimates, we used a species-specific calibration of G. ruber w., and an equation for mixed subsurface dwellers for G. truncatulinoides.
steps.searchable_summary: convert Mg/Ca ratios to temperature estimates using calibration equations

methods.description: Reconstruction of salinity changes and δ18O values using established procedures and corrections for ice volume.

steps.description: Changes in salinity are reconstructed following the procedure described in Repschläger et al. (2015).
steps.searchable_summary: reconstruct salinity changes using Repschläger procedure

steps.description: The temperature effect, estimated from the Mg/Ca was removed from the foraminiferal δ18O carbonate using the general equation of Shackleton (1974).
steps.searchable_summary: remove temperature effect from δ18O using Shackleton equation

steps.description: A correction for VPDB to SMOW was included.
The δ18O w records were corrected for ice volume, using the relative sea level composite curve of Waelbroeck (2002).
steps.searchable_summary: correct δ18O w for ice volume using Waelbroeck curve

methods.description: Reconstruction of the AF front using the relative abundance of G. ruber w. as a tracer for the position of the northern STG rim.

steps.description: Changes in the AF front were reconstructed using the relative abundance of G. ruber w. published by Weinelt et al., 2015.
steps.searchable_summary: reconstruct AF front using G. ruber w. abundance data
